title: Haymarket Crashes Books That ‘Meet the Moment’
summary: Haymarket Crashes Books That ‘Meet the Moment’

multimedia.alt: Haymarket Crashes Books That ‘Meet the Moment’

fullArticle:
Haymarket Books, the Chicago-based radical left-wing press known for focusing just as much on social and economic justice as on its bottom line, is marking its 25th anniversary with one of its best years in recent memory.

According to Haymarket publicity director Jim Plank, sales during the last fiscal year were up 22%, and three releases have landed on New York Times bestsellers lists since January 2025. Plank attributes the press's growth to it being “a publisher that continues to meet the moment."

Haymarket’s business model includes crashing books that address topical issues. Most recently, the press announced it would publish How to Close a Camp by the journalist John Washington on July 21. The book sets out strategies for individuals and groups who want to take action against the Tr...
